section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
section13_checks.check_rationale: No structural reference in content or traceability_footer identifies a Grant Agreement Annex, AGA Template, or Model Grant Agreement Annex as a schema source. The artifact's schema_id is orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1, derived from Tier 2A instrument schema authority. No strings matching 'Annex [0-9]', 'Grant Agreement Annex', 'AGA Template', or 'Model Grant Agreement template' used as a structural authority are present in section content or source references.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
section13_checks.check_rationale: All call requirement and scope claims in the artifact (SR-09, SR-10, CC-03, CC-04, CC-05, CC-07, CC-08) are explicitly attributed to Tier 2B sources in claim_statuses: C-09 cites scope_requirements.json SR-10 and call_constraints.json CC-05; C-10 cites scope_requirements.json SR-09 and call_constraints.json CC-03/CC-04; C-11 cites call_constraints.json CC-07. The traceability_footer.primary_sources includes docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json and docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json. No call-constraint claim lacks a Tier 2B source reference in the artifact's traceability structure.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, or equipment — not present in Tier 3
section13_checks.check_rationale: All named partners (ATU, BIIS, CERIA, NIHS, ISIA, ELI, FIIT, BAL), their stated capabilities, country affiliations, WP leads, and contribution lists are referenced in the traceability_footer.primary_sources via docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json and docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json (both Tier 3). Claim C-12 (confirmed) explicitly asserts all eight partners with capabilities and roles are sourced from Tier 3. Claim C-13 (confirmed) asserts per-partner WP contribution listings match Tier 4 partner_role_matrix which is itself derived from Tier 3 consortium data. No project-fact claim lacks a Tier 3 source reference in the artifact's traceability structure.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed
section13_checks.check_rationale: The artifact's schema_id 'orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1' (prefix 'orch.tier5.') identifies it as a Phase 8 Tier 5 artifact. Claim C-01 carries status 'confirmed' and asserts budget gate passed, citing source_ref 'Tier 4: phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json gate_pass_declaration'; this path also appears in traceability_footer.primary_sources. B.3.1 content states 'Resource allocation across all nine WPs is confirmed by the validated budget response; effort detail appears in Table 3.1f' — referencing validation without asserting raw budget figures. Scanning section content for budget-figure patterns: no instances of '€[0-9]+', '[0-9]+ EUR', or '[0-9]+ person-months' used as confirmed values were detected. Note: direct read of budget_gate_assessment.json is outside TAPM input boundary; budget gate passage is assessed from the artifact's own confirmed claim C-01 and its Tier 4 source reference.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
section13_checks.check_rationale: Not directly checkable by reading an artifact. This prohibition governs runtime agent behaviour — specifically whether decisions affecting future interpretation are written to docs/tier4_orchestration_state/decision_log/ rather than retained only in agent working memory. That behaviour cannot be verified from the static content or structure of a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. Recorded as pass per skill specification non-applicability rule for artifact-level checks of this type.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
section13_checks.check_rationale: Not directly checkable from artifact content. This prohibition governs the behavioural scope of workflow and skill components at runtime execution, not the content of a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. No artifact-level indicator of scope expansion is detectable. Recorded as pass per skill specification non-applicability rule for artifact-level checks of this type.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
section13_checks.check_rationale: This Phase 8 Tier 5 artifact's traceability_footer.primary_sources references only lower-numbered phases and tiers: Phase 3 (wp_structure.json), Phase 4 (gantt.json), Phase 6 (implementation_architecture.json), Phase 7 (budget_gate_assessment.json), Tier 3 consortium files, Tier 2A section schema registry, and Tier 2B call sources. All referenced upstream phase outputs follow canonical phase ordering (3→4→6→7→8). No forward-phase reference — a later-phase output used as an input to an earlier phase — is detected. No evidence of phase reordering or gate condition weakening is present in the artifact.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
section13_checks.check_rationale: validation_status.overall_status is 'inferred' (not 'unresolved'). Reviewing all 15 claim_status entries: 14 carry status 'confirmed' with named Tier 2–4 source references; 1 carries status 'inferred' (C-15: D5-02 healthcare dataset dissemination level SEN, sourced from Tier 4 implementation_architecture.json ethics_assessment ETHICS-02 with inference basis stated). No claim_status entry carries status 'unresolved'. No section content contains unresolved material without explicit flagging. The single inferred claim is properly declared with its inference chain. No violation of the finalization-without-flagging prohibition is detected.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
section13_checks.check_rationale: All regulatory-adjacent claims are attributed to Tier 2B call constraints rather than asserted as programme rules from agent knowledge: ADRA and GenAI4EU Hub cohesion (SR-09/CC-03/CC-04), AI-on-demand platform mandate (SR-10/CC-05), AI Factories and TEF linkages (CC-07), Switzerland associated country status (CC-08) — all cited explicitly with Tier 2B source identifiers. No direct assertion of Horizon Europe legal rules (participation conditions from the Framework Programme Regulation, financial regulation terms, grant agreement conditions) is made without a source reference. The traceability_footer contains no docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/ path, consistent with the absence of direct Tier 1 regulatory assertions in the content. No substitution of agent-local programme knowledge for accessible Tier 1 source documents is detected.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
section13_checks.check_rationale: Artifact is a Tier 5 deliverable. traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is true. All 15 claim_status entries are reviewed: no entry carries status 'unresolved'. The single inferred claim (C-15) is accompanied by a source_ref (Tier 4: implementation_architecture.json ethics_assessment ETHICS-02). All 14 confirmed claims carry named Tier 2 or Tier 3 or Tier 4 source references. The traceability_footer.primary_sources lists nine specific source paths spanning Tier 2A, Tier 2B, Tier 3, and Tier 4. No Tier 5 content claim is found to lack traceability to Tier 1–4 sources.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
section13_checks.check_rationale: Not checkable from a phase output or deliverable artifact. This prohibition governs write operations to files within docs/tier1_normative_framework/ and docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/ or docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/, which cannot be detected by inspecting a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. Recorded as pass per skill specification non-applicability rule for artifact-level checks of this type.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional
section13_checks.check_rationale: Not directly checkable from artifact content. This prohibition governs the interpretive stance of agents, skills, and workflows toward CLAUDE.md as the repository constitution, which cannot be assessed by examining the content of a Tier 5 deliverable artifact. Recorded as pass per skill specification non-applicability rule for artifact-level checks of this type.
